title: Welsh Scotch Egg
description: A twist on the classic British dish, this Welsh Scotch egg features lamb instead of sausage.
instructions: Hard-boil the egg and set aside.
In a pan, cook the lamb mince over medium heat until it reaches 74°C (165°F).
Add the breadcrumbs, paprika, salt, and pepper to the lamb mince and stir to combine.
Wrap the lamb mixture around the hard-boiled egg, making sure to seal the edges.
Dredge the lamb-coated egg in breadcrumbs to coat.
Deep fry the Welsh Scotch egg in hot oil until golden brown.
Drain the egg on paper towels and serve hot.
